brief: We repeatedly build small frontend tools and forget the same checks: inspect existing package scripts before adding dependencies; avoid landing pages when the request is for an app; start a dev server after implementation; verify desktop and mobile layout; summarize files changed and tests run. Create a Codex skill that helps future agents build practical frontend tools with these habits. It should include a validation checklist and avoid becoming a generic frontend tutorial.

Our team often receives GitHub PR review comments and loses time rediscovering the process. A useful skill should fetch unresolved review threads, separate actionable comments from discussion, patch only the requested files, run focused tests, reply with evidence, and avoid resolving threads unless the fix is actually implemented. It must be consent-based when pushing or editing PR state.

We need a skill for evaluating model output datasets. It should load JSONL cases, check schema drift, sample failures, compare baseline and candidate outputs, compute simple aggregate metrics, and produce a concise report. It should put deterministic parsing and scoring in scripts, while keeping interpretation guidance in references.

Design a self-evolving skill workflow. The skill may inspect past tasks, propose edits to itself or other skills, create experiments, and generate patches. It must not silently install or replace skills. It needs audit gates, a rollback story, and a way to prove that a proposed evolution improves future performance.
